item.title: Two national team setbacks drive Qaqa ahead of first Commonwealth Games
item.description: Qaqa will represent Fiji in the men's 110 metres hurdles at the Glasgow Commonwealth Games. He began his athletics journey competing in the high jump and long jump while attending LDS Church College in Tamavua before switching to hurdles after moving to Liahona High School in Tonga.
item.content:encoded:
Qaqa will represent Fiji in the men's 110 metres hurdles at the Glasgow Commonwealth Games.

He began his athletics journey competing in the high jump and long jump while attending LDS Church College in Tamavua before switching to hurdles after moving to Liahona High School in Tonga.

Qaqa says the disappointment of missing national selection twice continues to motivate him to train harder and improve.

Speaking to fijivillage News, he says preparations have been the toughest of his career because he will be competing at a much higher level, but the support of his teammates has helped him through the demanding training sessions.

He says the increased intensity of training has prepared the team to compete against some of the Commonwealth's best athletes.

Qaqa , who has paternal links to Qamea and maternal links to Naceva in Kadavu, says he is excited to represent Fiji at his first Commonwealth Games.

His main goal is to progress as far as possible in the competition, with hopes of advancing from the heats into the semi-finals and possibly the final.

He is also aiming to improve his personal best as he prepares for the Pacific Games in Tahiti next year.

Qaqa is encouraging young people to continue working towards their dreams, saying hard work can help them represent Fiji and achieve even greater success in the future.

The Glasgow Commonwealth Games will kick off today and end on the 2nd of August.

Qaqa will compete in the men's 110 metres hurdles during the evening session in Glasgow on Monday, 27th July. The event will be held from 5.30am to 9am on Tuesday, 28th of July.

item.title: Super W Grand Final in Fiji a landmark for women's sport - Coach
item.description: Hosting the first-ever Super W Grand Final in Fiji is a landmark moment for women's sport in the country and a significant boost for women's rugby. McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's head coach Mike Legge shared the comments from their home-base in Nadi, saying the occasion reflects the remarkable progress women's rugby has made in Fiji this year.
item.content:encoded:
Hosting the first-ever Super W Grand Final in Fiji is a landmark moment for women's sport in the country and a significant boost for women's rugby.

McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's head coach Mike Legge shared the comments from their home-base in Nadi, saying the occasion reflects the remarkable progress women's rugby has made in Fiji this year.

Legge says the team has earned its place in the final through hard work and dedication.

He believes one of the biggest factors behind the Drua's success has been the strong bond among the players and the positive culture within the squad.

He added that while the team has maintained high standards and worked hard throughout the season, the coaching staff has also focused on creating a relaxed and enjoyable environment, allowing the players to have fun and bring out their best performances.

When asked about the NSW Waratahs, Legge acknowledged the challenge they pose, noting that the Tahs are the back-to-back defending champions and are chasing a record seventh Super W title.

He says the players have great respect for the Waratahs and understand the quality and experience they will bring into the grand final.

The Fijian Drua will face the Waratahs at 1pm at the 4R Stadium at Govind Park in Ba on Saturday.

item.title: MGM High ready for Southern Zone finals showdown
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 23/07/2026 Mahatma Gandhi Memorial High School will be backing its Under-14 and Under-16 Deans teams and its Raluve Under-18 side as they aim to bring glory to the school in the Vodafone Deans Southern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union and Weetbix Raluve finals this Saturday.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 23/07/2026

Mahatma Gandhi Memorial High School will be backing its Under-14 and Under-16 Deans teams and its Raluve Under-18 side as they aim to bring glory to the school in the Vodafone Deans Southern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union and Weetbix Raluve finals this Saturday.

The Under-14 side will take on Suva Grammar School at 8.30am, while the Under-16 team will face Nasinu Secondary School.

The time for the Under-16 match is yet to be confirmed.

The Raluve Under-18 side will then meet Lomary Secondary School.

MGM High has continued to make its mark in sports, with its Under-17 team creating history by winning the 2023 Deans Under-17 title against Ratu Kadavulevu School.

The school has also enjoyed success in athletics, claiming back-to-back girls' division titles at the Coca-Cola Games.

In other finals, Marist Brothers High School will face Suva Grammar School in the Under-15 final, Gospel High School will take on Suva Grammar School in the Under-17 final, Nasinu Secondary School will meet Suva Grammar School in the Under-19 final, and Nasinu Secondary School will also face Suva Grammar School in the Under-18 final.

In the Weet-Bix Raluve Competition, Suva Grammar School will take on Nasinu Secondary School in the Under-16 final.

All matches will be played at the Suva Grammar Grounds and Veiuto Ground.

item.title: Fijian Drua ready to turn the tables on defending champions - Captain
item.description: The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's side will be looking to avenge their loss to the NSW Waratahs in round 1 when they meet this Saturday in the Super W Grand Final in Ba. Speaking from their home-base in Nadi, Captain Kolora Lomani says t he Waratahs are a quality side and they defeated them during the pool stages however, the final presents a completely different ball game.
item.content:encoded:
The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's side will be looking to avenge their loss to the NSW Waratahs in round 1 when they meet this Saturday in the Super W Grand Final in Ba.

Speaking from their home-base in Nadi, Captain Kolora Lomani says t he Waratahs are a quality side and they defeated them during the pool stages however, the final presents a completely different ball game.

She says the players are working hard to change that result.

Lomani says the Waratahs are the defending champions and they are well aware of the challenge they face this weekend.

She says the team has prepared thoroughly and is determined to deliver a strong performance in front of their home fans.

Lomani says they will maintain their brand of rugby and the game plan in place to counter the Waratahs, and she is urging fans to come out in numbers and cheer the team on.

She says the fans are the heartbeat of the team.

Lomani says her parents will be travelling from Australia to watch her for the final.

The Fijian Drua will face the Waratahs at 1pm at the 4R Stadium at Govind Park in Ba on Saturday.

item.title: Vunimono High U-17 edge DAV College 1-0 to maintain winning start
item.description: Vunimono High School's Under-17 side defeated DAV College 1-0 in the second match of the Fiji FA Southern Secondary Schools final play-Offs at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori. Both teams battled hard in an evenly contested first half.
item.content:encoded:
Vunimono High School's Under-17 side defeated DAV College 1-0 in the second match of the Fiji FA Southern Secondary Schools final play-Offs at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori.

Both teams battled hard in an evenly contested first half.

Vunimono has secured all three points and continue their winning start to the competition.

The competition continues at Ratu Cakobau Park, where teams will battle for the remaining qualification spots.

item.title: Vunimono High U15 thrash MGM 4-1 in Southern Secondary Schools final playoff
item.description: Vunimono High School's Under-15 side defeated Mahatama Gandhi Memorial High School 4-1 in the opening match of the Fiji FA Southern Secondary Schools Football Association final play-offs at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori this morning. Both teams created several scoring opportunities in an entertaining first half, with the scores locked at 1-1 at the break.
item.content:encoded:
Vunimono High School's Under-15 side defeated Mahatama Gandhi Memorial High School 4-1 in the opening match of the Fiji FA Southern Secondary Schools Football Association final play-offs at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori this morning.

Both teams created several scoring opportunities in an entertaining first half, with the scores locked at 1-1 at the break.

Vunimono raised their intensity after half-time, scoring three unanswered goals to seal an emphatic victory and make a strong start to their campaign.

The Southern Secondary Schools final play-offs bring together the top school teams from the Southern Zone, with the best-performing sides earning qualification for the Fiji FA Secondary Schools National Championship.

The competition continues at Ratu Cakobau Park, where teams will battle for the remaining qualification spots.

item.title: Young aiming to break personal best at first Commonwealth Games
item.description: The 26-year-old from Nakaseleka in Kadavu recently equalled her 100 metres personal best at the Oceania Championships and is determined to go one step further at the Games. Speaking to fijivillage Sports, Young says training has been the toughest it has ever been, but having a supportive group of athletes has helped everyone push through the demanding sessions.
item.content:encoded:
The 26-year-old from Nakaseleka in Kadavu recently equalled her 100 metres personal best at the Oceania Championships and is determined to go one step further at the Games.

Speaking to fijivillage Sports, Young says training has been the toughest it has ever been, but having a supportive group of athletes has helped everyone push through the demanding sessions.

She says the team constantly encourages one another, making the preparation more enjoyable.

Although this will be her first Commonwealth Games, Young has previously represented Fiji at the Commonwealth Youth Games.

She says competing against world-class athletes will be a big challenge, but believes racing against the best will bring out the best in her.

Young will compete in the women's 100 metres and 200 metres.

She also paid tribute to her training partner, Erol Qaqa, saying his positive attitude, work ethic and energy during training continue to inspire and motivate her, especially on the toughest days.

Young says she is taking her preparation one training session at a time while remaining focused on achieving a new personal best in Glasgow.

The Glasgow Commonwealth Games will kick off this Friday and end on the 2nd of August.

The women's 100 metres event is scheduled for Monday, 27th July , from 10am to 1pm, while the women's 200 metres will be held on Wednesday, 29th July , also from 10am to 1pm.

item.title: Junior Bula Boys to face New Zealand in OFC U-16 Championship final
item.description: The Junior Bula Boys will take on New Zealand in the final of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship this Saturday after both teams secured impressive semi-final victories and qualified for the 2027 FIFA U-17 World Cup. Fiji booked their place in the final after defeating the Solomon Islands 4-2 in a dramatic penalty shootout following a goalless semi-final while New Zealand secured their place in the fi
item.content:encoded:
The Junior Bula Boys will take on New Zealand in the final of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship this Saturday after both teams secured impressive semi-final victories and qualified for the 2027 FIFA U-17 World Cup.

Fiji booked their place in the final after defeating the Solomon Islands 4-2 in a dramatic penalty shootout following a goalless semi-final while New Zealand secured their place in the final with a convincing 3-0 win over hosts Papua New Guinea in the second semi-final.

Captain Jone Loga who was also named player of the match expresses his gratitude to God for the achievement and described qualifying for the FIFA U-17 World Cup as a dream come true.

Cruz Pietersen of New Zealand was named Player of the Match.

The OFC U-16 Men's Championship final between Fiji and New Zealand will be played at 9.30pm Saturday at PNG Football Stadium in Port Moresby.

item.title: Fijian Drua Women will play the same brand of rugby in the final - Coach
item.description: The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's team will remain committed to its trademark style of rugby when it takes on the New South Wales W

item.description: The Fiji Under-18 girls' Handball team has received a major boost for their preparation to the IHF Women's Youth World Championshi

item.title: Legge sticks with winning squad for Super W Final in Ba this Saturday
item.description: McDonald's Fiji Drua Women Head Coach Mike Legge has named an unchanged 23-member squad for Saturday's Super Rugby Women’s Grand F

item.title: Junior Bula Boys face Solomon Islands for place at 2027 FIFA U-17 World Cup
item.description: The Fiji U-16 side is just one win away from securing a place at a third consecutive FIFA U-17 World Cup as it takes on the Solomo

item.title: History comes home as Fijian Drua Women host first Super Rugby Women’s grand final in Ba
item.description: History will be made this Saturday when the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women host the Super Rugby Women's Grand Final on home soil for t
